answer: We have that $3^n \equiv 1 \pmod{143}$, so $3^n \equiv 1 \pmod{11}$ and $3^n \equiv 1 \pmod{13}$.  Since $3^5 \equiv 1 \pmod{11}$ and $3^4 \equiv 1 \pmod{13}$, we have that $3^{20} \equiv 1 \pmod{11}$ and $3^{12} \equiv 1 \pmod{13}$.  Therefore, $3^{60} \equiv 1 \pmod{143}$, so $3^{61} \equiv 3 \pmod{143}$, $3^{62} \equiv 9 \pmod{143}$, $3^{63} \equiv 27 \pmod{143}$, $3^{64} \equiv 81 \pmod{143}$, $3^{65} \equiv 43 \pmod{143}$, $3^{66} \equiv 29 \pmod{143}$, $3^{67} \equiv 87 \pmod{143}$, $3^{68} \equiv 61 \pmod{143}$, $3^{69} \equiv 83 \pmod{143}$, $3^{70} \equiv 49 \pmod{143}$, $3^{71} \equiv 47 \pmod{143}$, $3^{72} \equiv 41 \pmod{143}$, $3^{73} \equiv 23 \pmod{143}$, $3^{74} \equiv 69 \pmod{143}$, $3^{75} \equiv 7 \pmod{143}$, $3^{76} \equiv 21 \pmod{143}$, $3^{77} \equiv 63 \pmod{143}$, $3^{78} \equiv 89 \pmod{143}$, $3^{79} \equiv 67 \pmod{143}$, $3^{80} \equiv 1 \pmod{143}$.  Therefore, the least positive integer $n$ such that when $3^n$ is written in base $143$, its two right-most digits in base $143$ are $01$ is $\boxed{80}$.
